commands.review_summary:
The branch updates the bundled StepFun provider's onboarding catalog refresh, auth copy, auth-order region inference, model constants, and StepFun provider tests.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers: source comparison of PR head against current main shows missing manifest-backed descriptors and a standard/default 2603 policy change. I did not run live StepFun onboarding because this is a read-only review and the PR lacks real behavior proof.
commands.review_followup: Human review is needed because the branch is conflicting/stale, changes provider model policy, and lacks contributor real behavior proof.

target.title: Plugins/StepFun: refine onboarding UX
target.body:
## Summary

Follow-up to the bundled StepFun provider work from #60032.

This tightens the StepFun onboarding experience in three places:

- refreshes managed StepFun catalogs during onboarding so stale StepFun models do not keep leaking into later picker flows
- clarifies the auth copy so the StepFun Standard API and Step Plan paths are easier to distinguish
- **improves implementation robustness** by fixing fragile string parsing and unreliable object traversal

## What changed

### Core UX improvements
- refresh both `stepfun` and `stepfun-plan` managed catalogs when StepFun onboarding rewrites config
- strip stale managed StepFun model ids before reapplying the current managed catalogs
- prefer the latest configured StepFun region when multiple old region-tagged profiles still exist
- make the auth grouping and prompt copy more explicit:
  - `Standard API / Step Plan (China / Global)`
  - prompts now call out standard vs Step Plan endpoints directly

### Technical fixes
- **Fix fragile string parsing**: Replace `STEPFUN_DEFAULT_MODEL_REF.split("/")[1]` with direct `STEPFUN_DEFAULT_MODEL_ID` constant reference for better robustness
- **Fix unreliable object traversal**: Simplify region inference to rely only on explicit `auth.order` array instead of undeterministic `Object.entries()` traversal order
- **Improve naming consistency**: Use "Global" instead of "International" for better clarity and remove confusing "Global/Intl" labels

### Testing
- add focused regression coverage for onboarding cleanup and region selection
- all existing tests pass

## Why

The initial bundled provider landed correctly, but there were still a couple of UX footguns and implementation fragilities:

**UX issues:**
- old managed StepFun model entries could survive re-onboarding and show confusing choices in later model pickers
- the auth wording still left some ambiguity between StepFun Standard API and Step Plan setup paths
- "International" terminology was less clear than "Global"

**Implementation issues:**
- region inference relied on string parsing that could break if format changed
- region fallback logic depended on undeterministic object property traversal order

## Validation

- `pnpm test -- extensions/stepfun/onboard.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.stepfun.test.ts`
- `pnpm test -- src/scripts/docs-link-audit.test.ts`
- `pnpm test -- src/plugins/provider-auth-choices.test.ts`
- `pnpm test -- src/plugins/manifest-registry.test.ts`
- `docker build -t openclaw:stepfun-onboard-followup-branch -f Dockerfile .`

## AI Assistance

This PR includes AI-assisted improvements focused on implementation robustness and code quality.
